english: This is a common Chinese surname. Written as 趙, it is the 7th most common surname in China (Mainland China and Taiwan), especially among the Mandarin-speaking regions. A 2013 survey finds that 趙 is the 9th most common surname in Mainland China, used by 26.7 million people. 趙 is listed in the number one spot in the Hundred Family Surnames, because it happens to be the surname of the emperor during the Song Dynasty (AD 960-1279) when the list was compiled. In Hokkien, 趙 is spelled Teo, and may resemble another surname Teoh but written as 張 in Chinese characters. In Penang, the surname 趙 is usually spelled as Teo among the Hokkien. In Cantonese, it is Chao. It is cognate with the Vietnamese family name Triệu and the Korean family name Cho (조). In Mandarin, it is written as Zhào and shares the same romanised spelling with another, much rarer surname Zhào (兆).
This is a Chinese surname. It is actually not pronounced teoh4 but rather tniau1. The early spelling attempt has modified the resulting pronunciation.
